summary: Matched Koopa/Bowser special-move code centered on src/melee/ft/chara/ftKoopa/ftKp_SpecialS.c, with a large implementation expansion, tighter Fighter_GObj prototypes, Koopa motion-var/attribute type fixes, a new ftKp_SpecialS.h declaration header, and a table-typer opseq command created to locate assembly opcode-sequence patterns. The PR body specifically notes that ftKp_SpecialLw_80134ACC was difficult and that recognizing a cmpwi,beq,bge,cmpwi,beq,bge,b pattern as switch-generated, not if-generated, was key to reaching 100%.
what_changed: Expanded ftKp_SpecialS.c substantially, replacing partial/stubbed code with matched C for many Koopa special-state callbacks and helpers.
Factored shared side-special direction/input logic into static s32 ftKp_SpecialS_set_dir(Fighter_GObj* gobj), reused by ftKp_SpecialSHit_IASA, ftKp_SpecialAirSHit_IASA, and wait IASA paths.

Added or exposed numerous Koopa special callback declarations in ftKp_SpecialS.h, including SpecialS hit/wait/end Phys and Coll callbacks, SpecialLw animation/physics/collision helpers, SpecialN physics/end animation callbacks, and return-type-correct SpecialLw helpers.
Retyped ftKp_Init.h prototypes from HSD_GObj* to Fighter_GObj* throughout the Koopa init/special declarations, and changed ftKp_SpecialLw_80134DE0 and ftKp_SpecialLw_80134E1C from void to s32.
Extended union ftKoopa_MotionVars.specials with x10, x14, and x18 s32 fields and adjusted ftKoopaAttributes x4 and x20 from u32 to s32.
Added ftKp_Unk1.h declarations for extern enum_t ftKp_Init_803CF2A0[], with reviewer caveat that the file may not represent a real source split.

Added table-typer opseq subcommand to search asm files for comma-separated opcode sequences, optionally filtering to 100% matched functions by reading build/GALE01/report.json and sorting results by function size.
smart_moves: Used tooling rather than manual inspection for a hard matching problem: the new table-typer opseq command let the author grep assembly opcode sequences such as cmpwi,beq,bge,cmpwi,beq,bge,b across the project.
Inferred code shape from opcode patterns: the author found that the beq,bge,b pattern was caused by switch statements, not if chains, which directly guided the successful C rewrite.
Reused shared input-direction logic via ftKp_SpecialS_set_dir instead of duplicating the same B-button and stick-threshold handling in ground and aerial IASA callbacks.
Improved type precision by using Fighter_GObj* in fighter-specific APIs, which can reduce casts and help future matching and header consistency.
Made motion-variable layout changes in types.h alongside the matched code, preserving offsets for fields used by the new implementation.
decomp_lessons: When a stubborn function has repeated cmpwi/branch patterns, search for similar opcode sequences in already matched code to infer whether the compiler likely emitted a switch rather than nested if/else logic.
Small purpose-built tooling can pay off during decomp: opseq gave a reusable way to compare assembly idioms across the codebase instead of relying on local intuition.
For fighter code, prefer Fighter_GObj* in prototypes when the function is fighter-specific; broad HSD_GObj* declarations can hide type information needed for matching and readability.
Header and struct changes should accompany matching work when return types, motion-variable fields, or signedness are revealed by the matched implementation.
Temporary unknown headers may be acceptable to unblock matching, but reviewer feedback should be preserved because the data may later need relocation into the real owning file.
naming_conventions: Koopa fighter symbols use the ftKp_ prefix, e.g. ftKp_SpecialS_Enter, ftKp_SpecialAirS_Enter, ftKp_SpecialSHit_IASA.
Ground/aerial variants are distinguished with SpecialS versus SpecialAirS in function names.
State-callback suffixes follow the project convention _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll.
Unidentified functions retain address-suffixed names such as ftKp_SpecialS_80132E30 and ftKp_SpecialLw_80134ACC.
New local helper naming in this PR used descriptive static names such as ftKp_SpecialS_set_dir and inline helper names such as ftKp_SpecialSWait_IASA_inline.
Header guard for the new ftKp_SpecialS.h used an address-style guard, GALE01_80134288, matching existing decomp header patterns.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added table-typer opseq usage: table-typer opseq cmpwi,beq,bge,cmpwi,beq,bge,b.
The opseq command rewrites the comma-separated opcode list into asm-line substrings prefixed with the comment/opcode formatting and scans asm files for consecutive matching lines.
The opseq command reads build/GALE01/report.json, filters to 100% matched functions by default via -m, and sorts results by matched function size.
The PR body records the key matching insight: cmpwi/beq/bge followed by beq/bge/b corresponded to switch statements in observed matched code, not if statements.
The author wanted a future richer opseq syntax capable of matching registers, wildcards, and repeated branch targets, but that was described as future work rather than implemented here.
Used PAD_STACK(16) in rewritten IASA callbacks where needed to preserve stack/codegen shape.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: I don't think this is a "real" file, and its data probably just belongs in KpSpecialS, but this is totally fine for now
review_feedback.lesson: ftKp_Unk1.h should be treated as a temporary organizational location for ftKp_Init_803CF2A0; future cleanup may move the data declaration into KpSpecialS or another real owning header/source.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that move ftKp_Init_803CF2A0 or remove ftKp_Unk1.h.
Search for later enhancements to table-typer opseq supporting wildcards, register matching, or repeated branch-target constraints.
Compare other fighter special matching PRs that changed HSD_GObj* prototypes to Fighter_GObj*.
Look for subsequent cleanup of ftKp_SpecialS.h header guard GALE01_80134288 or declaration placement.
Search for other uses of cmpwi,beq,bge switch-pattern matching in difficult functions.
